registration.graduating_exception: Graduating students can register for fewer than 16 credits.
registration.low_cgpa_restriction: Students with CGPA < 4.0 restricted to max 16 credits.
registration.add_drop_window: First 3 instructional days of the semester.
registration.withdrawal: Allowed for 3 days after CAT-I results are published. Grade 'W' is recorded. Cannot withdraw below 16 credits.
registration.backlogs: 'F' (Fail) or 'N' (Not Complete/Debarred) requires re-registration in next available semester.
registration.categories: FC (Foundation Core), DC (Discipline Core), DE (Discipline Elective), OE (Open Elective)

attendance.minimum_required_note: 75% minimum attendance per course is required to be eligible to write exams (CAT and FAT).
attendance.nine_pointer_exemption: YES — Students with CGPA >= 9.0 AND no active backlogs are EXEMPTED from the 75% attendance rule. Their eligibility is dynamically calculated per exam.
attendance.consequence: Students below 75% attendance (who don't qualify for the 9-pointer exemption) are NOT permitted to write the FAT for that course. Grade 'N' (Not Complete/Debarred) is awarded, equivalent to 0 grade points.
attendance.condonation: There is NO general condonation of attendance. The 75% rule is strictly enforced unless the 9-pointer exemption applies.
attendance.cgpa_exemption: Students with CGPA >= 9.0 and no backlogs are exempted from the 75% attendance requirement. This is the ONLY CGPA-based exemption.
attendance.calculation: Attendance = (Classes Attended / Total Classes Conducted) × 100, calculated per course independently.
attendance.lab_vs_theory: Attendance is tracked separately for theory and lab components. Both must independently meet 75% (unless 9-pointer exemption applies).
attendance.on_duty: OD (On Duty) for university-approved activities counts as attendance. Must be approved by Dean/HoD in advance.
attendance.medical: No automatic condonation for medical leave. Even with documentation, if attendance falls below 75% without the 9-pointer exemption, the student may be debarred.

assessment.split: 60% CAM (Continuous Assessment Mode) + 40% FAT (Final Assessment Test)

passing_criteria.note: Must score >= 40% in Theory FAT, >= 50% in Lab/Project, AND >= 50% overall (CAM+FAT) to pass.

grading_system.relative: Used for theory courses with >10 students. Class average = midpoint of B grade.
grading_system.absolute: Used for labs, projects, soft skills, and courses with <=10 students. S>=90, A>=80, B>=70, C>=60, D>=55, E>=50.
grading_system.s_grade_rule: To get S in relative grading: score >= (Mean + 1.5 × Std Dev) AND >= 90% absolute. If no one qualifies, max 3 students (or top 5%) get S.
grading_system.n_grade: 'N' grade is awarded when debarred from FAT due to attendance shortage or academic malpractice. Equivalent to 0 grade points.

assessment.hackathon_bonus: Up to 20 bonus CAM marks for participation in 24+ hour Hackathons/Make-a-thons.

final_project.eligibility: Allowed in pre-final semester if 65% of total credits are completed.

minors_honours.note: Requires 18 additional credits and average CGPA >= 7.5 in those specific courses.

credentials.course_substitution: Failed DE/OE courses can be substituted with another from the same basket (costs a fee).
credentials.grade_improvement: Can re-register a passed course to improve the grade. Max 1 per semester, unless graduating.
credentials.audit_courses: B.Tech students with CGPA >= 8.0 can audit up to 2 courses total. 'U' grade, does not affect CGPA.
credentials.pg_courses: UG students with CGPA >= 8.0 can register for PG-level courses under OE option.
credentials.ug_research: Students can take 3-credit research/project courses (TARP, Design Project, Simulation Project) to fulfill DE/OE requirements.

exam_absences.re_cat: Permitted for approved internships, fellowships, placement activities, or hospitalization.
exam_absences.re_fat: Granted ONLY for hospitalization (>1 week) or family death. Must apply within 14 days of the FAT.

re_evaluation.process: Pay to view FAT answer scripts. Apply for re-evaluation.
re_evaluation.threshold: If marks change by >5%, a third examiner checks (best of 3 taken).
re_evaluation.appeal: Final disputes escalated to the Examination Appellate Committee.

prerequisites_retakes.prerequisite_fail: If a student fails a prerequisite course ('F'), they ARE still permitted to register for the next level course.
prerequisites_retakes.component_retake: If 'N' grade because one component failed (e.g. passed theory, failed lab), only re-register the failed component. If 'F' (whole course fail), must re-register all components.

malpractice.cat_cheating: 0 marks for ALL subjects registered that semester for that exam component.
malpractice.fat_cheating: 'N' grade for ALL regular courses taken that semester.
